article_title: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Personal Finance Preview With a Plaid-Powered Dashboard for US Pro Users
summary: Archive duplicate of PR #1284, already published on 2026-05-16 as 'OpenAI Opens ChatGPT Personal Finance Preview to US Pro Users With Read-Only Plaid Access to 12,000 Institutions'. Same event, same launch day, same Plaid integration, same Pro-only US scope, same Hiro cross-reference. The topic:claim atomic gate did not catch the collision because the slug keyword sets differed enough. Per v3.9.0 workflow, the duplicate work is discarded.

findings.message: Archive duplicate — same event already published as 2026-05/16-openai-opens-chatgpt-personal-finance-preview-to-us-pro-users-with-read-only-plaid-access-to-12000-institutions (PR #1284)
findings.details: Both submissions cover the same May 15 OpenAI rollout of ChatGPT Personal Finance to US Pro subscribers via Plaid integration with 12,000+ institutions. PR #1284 was reviewed APPROVE on 2026-05-16T17:33 and merged into main shortly after. Per the v3.9.0 workflow, a duplicate of an already-published article is grounds for REJECT — there is no rewrite path. The work in this submission is discarded; future coverage of related developments (Plus rollout, Intuit integration, international expansion) would be a fresh event.

editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT for archive duplicate. The published article (PR #1284) covers the identical news event: OpenAI's May 15 launch of ChatGPT Personal Finance preview for US Pro subscribers via Plaid integration with 12,000+ institutions, with read-only access to balances/transactions/investments/liabilities, the Hiro acqui-hire cross-reference, the @Finances UX, the Intuit roadmap. Both articles cite TechCrunch, MacRumors, and Engadget for overlapping facts. The 1291 submission has minor additional content — Plaid corporate blog (Ty Geri + Will Robinson quotes), GPT-5.5 model branding via SiliconANGLE + TestingCatalog, conflicting benchmark numbers, Ethan Bloch attribution. None of these distinguishing details rise to 'genuine new development on the same story' — they are different framings of the same event. Per the write-article skill: 'Same event → pick a different topic. Do not re-cover.' Per the review-submission skill: 'The article is a duplicate of, or substantially overlaps with, an already-published article' → REJECT.

overlap_specifics: Same event: OpenAI Personal Finance preview launch on May 15
Same scope: ChatGPT Pro subscribers in US, web + iOS
Same institution count: 12,000+ (Schwab, Fidelity, Chase, Robinhood, Amex, Capital One)
Same read-only constraint and dashboard surfaces (spending, subscriptions, portfolio, upcoming payments)

editor_notes.root_cause: The topic:claim atomic gate (npm run topic:claim, v3.12.0) did not catch the collision because the slug keyword sets diverged: PR #1284 generated 'openai-chatgpt-personal-finance-12000' style keywords while PR #1291 generated 'openai-chatgpt-plaid-dashboard-pro'. The slug hash differed enough that both claims won server-side. Both agents launched ~10 seconds apart in the same parallel batch on 2026-05-16T17:14-17:18Z.
editor_notes.content_quality: On its own merits, the 1291 submission is well written — every specific in the article is verbatim against accessible snapshots (TechCrunch, Engadget, MacRumors, SiliconANGLE, TestingCatalog, Plaid blog). Quotes attributed to Ty Geri ('Finances are deeply personal...') and Will Robinson ('Bringing it to life requires three things...') are verbatim in the Plaid blog. But duplicate status overrides content quality.
concerns: Topic-claim gate (v3.12.0) did not prevent the collision because the slug hashes differed despite identical underlying news. A future improvement could compute slug similarity (e.g. Jaccard on keyword set + title) at claim time and reject claims that exceed a threshold against the slugs of open submission PRs.
One distinguishing claim in 1291 — the Ethan Bloch / Digit founder attribution — is not in the 1284 article. This is a minor additional fact, not a separate event.
recommendations: Consider tightening the topic:claim gate to include slug-similarity comparison against open submission-PR slugs (not just exact slug-hash match).

content_preview.title: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Personal Finance Preview With a Plaid-Powered Dashboard for US Pro Users
content_preview.summary: OpenAI began rolling out a personal finance experience inside ChatGPT on May 15, letting US Pro subscribers connect over 12,000 institutions through Plaid and see a unified dashboard of spending, subscriptions, portfolio and upcoming payments.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenAI on May 15 began rolling out a personal finance experience inside ChatGPT that lets users link their real bank, brokerage and credit card accounts and ask the assistant questions grounded in their actual money, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/15/openai-launches-chatgpt-for-personal-finance-will-let-you-connect-bank-accounts/). The preview is limited to ChatGPT Pro subscribers in the United States and is available on the web and on iOS, per [MacRumors](...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
